summary: Admin Notification Badges feature VERIFIED. FIXED bug in AdminLayout.js where token was read from 'token' instead of 'access_token'. All 14 backend tests pass. Frontend badge manager now initializes correctly with session-based baselines. Badges show NEW items since session started (current - baseline), which means badges are 0 on fresh login as expected.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED: AdminLayout.js line 202 used 'token' but app stores as 'access_token' - Changed to 'access_token'
Session-based badge design is working correctly:
- On login: current counts become baselines
- Badges appear when new items arrive during session
- Clicking section updates that baseline to current
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin notification badges feature is complete. Key implementation: useBadgeManager hook in AdminLayout.js fetches counts from /api/v1/admin/notification-counts and stores baselines in sessionStorage. Badges show NEW items (current - baseline). On fresh login, badges are 0 because baselines equal current counts. Polling interval is 30 seconds. Logout clears session storage.
features_verified: Endpoint requires admin authentication (401 without token) - VERIFIED
Response contains all required fields (kyc_pending, transfers_pending, card_requests_pending, tickets_unread, users_pending) - VERIFIED
Frontend sidebar shows nav items for Users, KYC Queue, Card Requests, Transfers Queue, Support Tickets - VERIFIED
Session-based badge baselines stored in sessionStorage - VERIFIED
Clicking menu item updates baseline for that section - VERIFIED
Logout clears session storage (admin_badge_baselines, admin_badge_session_id) - VERIFIED
Badge count formula: max(0, current - baseline) - VERIFIED

bug_fixed.issue: Token read from localStorage.getItem('token') but app stores token as 'access_token'
bug_fixed.impact: Badge manager was not initializing because token was null
